question: Does the application contain all required attachments listed in the guidance?
lookFor: Attachment 1: Service Area Map and Table
Attachment 4: Position Descriptions for Key Management Staff
Attachment 5: Biographical Sketches for Key Management Staff
Attachment 13: Health Center Program Requirements Compliance

conditionalAttachments.name: Attachment 6: Co-Applicant Agreement
conditionalAttachments.description: Required for public center applicants that have a co-applicant board

conditionalAttachments.name: Attachment 7: Summary of Contracts and Agreements
lookFor: Attachment 7: Summary of Contracts and Agreements

conditionalAttachments.name: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status
lookFor: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status

complianceGuidance: Per User Guide section 2.3.2, the following attachments are required for ALL application types (Type 1 New, Type 2 Competing Continuation, Type 3 Supplemental): Attachment 1 (Service Area Map and Table), Attachment 2 (Bylaws), Attachment 3 (Project Organizational Chart), Attachment 4 (Position Descriptions for Key Management Staff), Attachment 5 (Biographical Sketches for Key Management Staff), Attachment 9 (Collaboration Documentation), Attachment 10 (Sliding Fee Discount Schedule(s)), and Attachment 13 (Health Center Program Requirements Compliance). Attachment 6 (Co-Applicant Agreement) is required for public center applicants with a co-applicant board. Attachments 7 (Summary of Contracts and Agreements), 8 (Articles of Incorporation), 11 (Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status), and 12 (Operational Plan) are required only for Type 1 (New) applicants. All other attachments are as applicable or optional. The application must include all required attachments for the applicant's type/status to be considered complete.
description: Checks that all required attachments are present based on application type and applicant status.

question: Is the applicant an eligible entity as mentioned in the guidance (e.g. non-profit, governmental unit)?
complianceGuidance: The applicant must be an eligible entity as defined in the User Guide: a public or private nonprofit entity, including tribal, faith-based, or community-based organizations, or a governmental unit. The applicant's entity type must align with the type entered in SAM.gov and selected in Form 1A - General Information Worksheet. If 'Other' is selected, the applicant must specify the organization type. Tribal or Urban Indian entities that meet the definition for a public or private entity should select the Tribal or Urban Indian category. The applicant must not be a for-profit entity.
description: Verifies that the applicant is an eligible entity type (public or private nonprofit, governmental unit, etc.)
